div.h1: HTTP/1.1 Keep-Alive vs HTTP/1.0: FD Reuse and State Machine

g.text: Quantitative Comparison: 10K Clients x 100 Requests Each
HTTP/1.1: 10K clients hold 10K fds → 55,536 fds free for files/timers/pipes
HTTP/1.0: 10K rps x 60s TIME_WAIT = 600K phantom fds → EXHAUSTED in seconds
TIME_WAIT: OS holds fd in TIME_WAIT ~2*MSL (60s) after close() — fd unusable
SO_REUSEADDR: allows bind() reuse but does NOT free TIME_WAIT fds faster
tcp_tw_reuse=1: allows reuse for outgoing connections only, not listening
Keep-alive eliminates TIME_WAIT accumulation: fd never closes between requests
NGINX default: keepalive_timeout 65s — matches typical browser idle behavior
conn alloc/free: 3 x 26 KB = 78 KB churn
10K clients = 10K active fds at ALL times
Burst: new fd per request → fd exhaustion risk
fd exhaustion at 10K rps: YES (ulimit hit)
TIME_WAIT sockets block fd reuse for ~60s
fd=7 stays in epoll interest set
0 bytes heap freed, 0 syscalls for new connection
REACTOR_READ fires — new data on same fd
